title: Uniface 10.4.01: New Features Since 10.3.02
text: Uniface 10.4 .01: New Features Since 10.3.02 Uniface 10.4 .01 consolidates new features that have been provided in patches since 10.3.02. Documentation was updated for the following new features. IDE Features Context-sensitive help for ProcScript commands It is now possible to get context-sensitive help about ProcScript commands. When writing code in the script editor, press F1 to display the documentation topic about the current command. This will also work for triggers, predefined operation names, and precompiler directives. By default, the topic is displayed in a web browser. If you prefer to use the local compiled help ( ulibary.chm ), set the logical DOCUMENTATION_LOCATION to chm: in the ide.asn file and that the most recent ulibrary.chm and ulibrary.ini are installed in the /uniface/doc directory of your installation. For more information, see Uniface IDE Logicals . Smart suggestions and filtering extended to the Resource Browser When using the Resource Browser to add templates, objects, and snippets to entities, components, and libraries, you can start typing a keyword to automatically get smart suggestions and filter available contents, making it easier to navigate large repositories. For more information, see Smart Suggestions and Filtering and Filter and Search for Objects in Resource Browser . Write Script Worksheet has been enhanced. The Write Script worksheet of the Component Editor has been enhanced: You can now edit objects in the Structure of the Write Script worksheet. For example, you can rename or delete fields. Except for Form and Report components, you can also add objects using the new Template and Model tabs in the Resource Browser, and you can use standard editing functionality such as insert, copy, and paste. It is easier to split the Script Editor into two views, making it consistent with other Write Script worksheets. For more information, see Write Script Worksheet and Split Views in the Script Editor . Disable line numbering in Script Editor. To turn line numbering off, you can edit the SourceEdit widget definition in usys.ini by adding the option gutters=fold . This can improve performance when a code container has a large amount of code. For more information, see Configure Line Numbering in the Script Editor . Updated Start Page The Uniface Start Page has been updated with a cleaner look and more functionality. You can now: Create a new project and open utilities directly from the Start Page. For more information, see Create a Project . Review and go to the most recently posted questions about the IDE on Rocket Community . Directly open the most recently used objects. Go to documentation, Uniface eLearning, and related Uniface web sites. Tip: To refresh the Community Questions in the Start Page, or refresh the current editor, you can click the new Refresh button, or choose Action > Refresh . Updated Uniface templates The Uniface default templates have been enhanced. Object descriptions have been updated and Field templates restyled for usability. The default component palettes feature an entity called ENT.HIDDENINPALETTE which contains all other templates for fields and entities. Any entity with the suffix HIDDENINPALETTE is hidden when the palette is displayed in the Resource Browser. IDE command-line compilation can now return an exit code of 1 when errors are found Uniface now returns a Windows exit code of 0 ( EXIT_SUCCESS ) or 1 ( EXIT_FAILURE ) when compiling Global ProcScript libraries from the command line (using /lib ). For more information, see Compiling from the IDE Command Line . Compiling palettes and templates You can use the new compile sub-switch /iap to compile all development objects regardless of their purpose (Normal, Palettes, and Templates). It is also possible to do so in ProcScript using the IncludeAllPurposes options with $ude("compile") . For more information, see /iap and $ude ("compile") . Feature: The Load Definitions facility is now available for Informix The Load Definitions facility enables you to create modeled entities using the data dictionary of your existing database. For more information, see Load Definitions Facility . Web and Mobile Features Updated CEF version Uniface makes use of HTML rendering and JavaScript execution provided by the Chromium Embedded Framework (CEF3). Uniface has now upgraded to CEF version 81.3.10 with corresponding Chromium version 81.0.4044.138. This ensures that the HTML and JavaScript embedded in Uniface applications benefit from the latest security updates. This CEF version also makes it possible to run modern JavaScript frameworks and to rotate embedded PDF documents. To ensure that applications that make use of form containers and HTML widgets render smoothly, the PaintCache initialization setting has been re-introduced in the [upi] section. By default, it is set to True even if it is not specified in the initialization file. For more information, see PaintCache . New ProcScript function for setting client-side locale in DSPs. A new ProcScript function, $webinfo("Locale") , enables you to set the client-side locale in a DSP application. This is used to apply locale-based formatting to Date, Datetime, Time data instead of using the default English locale ( "en" ). For more information, see $webinfo("Locale") . New web triggers for custom reporting of server-side validation errors In dynamic server pages (DSPs) it is now possible to define field-level web triggers for reporting and clearing validation errors returned from the server. Uniface provides default reporting of validation errors, but this can be added to or replaced using the showError and clearError triggers. For more information, see View and Data Layers of Dynamic Server Pages , webtrigger showError , and webtrigger clearError . Enhancement for showError and clearError web triggers Activating a server-side operation from within the showError or clearError web triggers, now throws a uniface.UnifaceError exception. For more information, see webtrigger showError , and webtrigger clearError . DSP htmlinput widget of type text now supports selected display formats for Numeric and Float data In dynamic server pages, it is now possible to use format codes 9 , Z , P and K for Numeric and Float fields associated with the htmlinput widget of type text (such as the EditBox logical widget). Uniface returns the appropriate warnings or errors for invalid display formats, and when data cannot be formatted for display for deformatted for storage. For more information, see Display Formats for Numeric and Float Data in Dynamic Server Pages . Display formats $NLS(Numeric) and $NLS(Float) in DSPs Display formats $NLS(Numeric) and $NLS(Float) are now supported in DSPs for fields with Numeric or Float data type and the htmlinput widget of type text . For more information, see Display Formats for Numeric and Float Data in Dynamic Server Pages . New DSP widget property to enable server-side formatting for non-String fields By default, for a Uniface field with a non-string data type (Numeric/Float/Date/Time/DateTime/Boolean), data is formatted on the DSP client. You can now disable this behavior, and have the server handle all formatting and syntax checking by setting the widget property Data Formatting on Server ( serverDataFormatting ). This is the new name for a previously undocumented property: datatype=string . This property continues to work for compatibility purposes. For more information, see Data Formatting on Server (serverDataFormatting) . Open the uproperties.ini , located in UnifaceInstallDir/uniface/adm . In the section [propertygroups] , add the property serverdataformatting to grp:uniface_all : [propertygroups]...grp:uniface_all=grp:uniface_colors;clientsyntaxcheck;serverdataformatting ... Copy Add the following property specification at the end of the file: [serverdataformatting]description=Data Formatting on Serverdatatype=booleantooltip=Format the field data on the server in string typecategory=Uniface Copy New NumberSpinnerBox widget for DSPs A new widget is available for Numeric fields dynamic server pages. The NumberSpinnerBox is mapped to an HTML input control of type number . It can be used for Numeric fields that have a maximum and minimum value (specified by in the ValRep property). In browsers that support the number input type, the field is represented as an edit box with buttons to adjust the value. To enable this widget in the Uniface IDE: Edit common/adm/usys.ini and add the following line to the [webwidgets] section: NumberSpinnerBox=htmlinput(html:type=number) Copy To get the new template in DSP component editor, start the IDE, choose Load Templates from the Main Menu ( ≡ ), and then click Load default templates . For more information, see NumberSpinnerBox and htmlinput . Support for numeric ranges in DSPs Uniface provides support for numeric ranges in Dynamic Server pages. A range can be specified in the ValRep property of a Numeric field using the syntax Min : Max , it can be Set in ProcScript, or it set in JavaScript using the new API functions field.setValrep() and field.setValrepArray() . For more information, see setValrep() and setValrepArray() . The way any ValRep is represented, whether it is a text list or a numeric range, depends on the widget. It is the developer's responsibility to ensure that the ValRep matches the requirements of the widget. For more information, see ValRep Lists and Ranges and ValRep Format error when data type and htmlinput type are incompatible If the HTML control generated by the DSP htmlinput widget is unable to correctly format the data for its input type, a format error results and an exception is displayed in the web browser's console. For more information on supported data types and how data is formatted for each htmlinput type, see htmlinput . Display format $NLS( FormatPattern ) in DSPs The display format $NLS( FormatPattern ) is now supported in DSPs for fields with Numeric or Float data type and the htmlinput widget of type text . This makes is possible to specify the number of digits after the decimal point. For more information, see Display Formats for Numeric and Float Data in Dynamic Server Pages . Client-side formatting errors in DSPs no longer result in blank data fields For non-String fields using an EditBox widget, if a value cannot displayed in the specified display format, it is now displayed with asterisks instead of leaving the field blank. A format error continues to be logged in the browser console. For more information, see Display Formats for Numeric and Float Data in Dynamic Server Pages and Display Formats for Date and Time in Dynamic Server Pages . New servlet setting to control size of data in client HTML and SOAP requests To protect against some types of Denial of Service attack, you can limit the size of data sent in POST and PUT requests by setting the servlet setting MAXPOSTDATASIZE . By default, the data content of a request is limited to 2 MB. For more information, see MAXPOSTDATASIZE , Uniface WRD Configuration Errors and Customize WRD Error Messages . New Servlet Setting: TCPKEEPALIVE It is now possible to configure the WRD and SRD servlets to keep a persistent TCP connection with the Uniface Router. The TCPKEEPALIVE setting is analogous to the ka option of the TCP connector. It works on the TCP connection between the servlet and the Uniface Router. For more information, see TCPKEEPALIVE . All DSP widgets now support custom HTML attributes Dynamic server page widgets support any syntactically valid HTML attribute name when specified as html: AttributeName . These are converted to HTML attributes and the browser determines how they are handled. For more information, see Widget Properties for the Dynamic Server Page and Setting DSP Widget Properties . Desktop Application Features You can now dynamically change the Position property of popup forms using $windowproperties For more information, see $windowproperties and Position . Enhancements for right-to-left (RTL ) desktop application development This patch features several enhancements for RTL application development: The assignment setting $RTL_APPLICATION now works on left-to-right (LTR) systems, and not just on right-to-left (RTL) systems. For more information, see $RTL_APPLICATION . The direction of form components (as specified by the Direction window property) and fields (in the Field Layout ) is now applied on a left-to-right system. Previously, it was only applied when the application was running on a right-to-left system. For more information, see Direction and Field Layout . Menus are correctly mirrored, including the right-align marker. Fields, entities, and area frames that are attached to the left or right window border are now mirrored when run as RTL application. Thus, if the object's Attach to Window Border (Attach) property is set to Left, the widget, entity, or area frame is attached to the right in an RTL application. For more information, see Attach to Window Border (attach) . These changes make it possible to develop an application on a left-to-right system (the Uniface IDE only runs LTR) and run, test, and deploy on both LTR and RTL systems. For more information, see Desktop Applications for Bidirectional Languages . TLS Features The UROUTMON component and Uniface Router Monitor now support a TLS connection to the Uniface Router. To enable TLS support, a number of changes have been made to the UROUTMON component, and these have consequences: The CONNECT operation of the UROUTMON component has been updated to support longer connection strings. For more information, see CONNECT . If you have created your own monitor using the UROUTMON component, you may need to enlarge the connection address field, used for connecting to the Uniface Router, to allow TLS profile names. If you use the Uniface Router Monitor application provided in the distribution, be aware that the connection address will only show a maximum of 2 characters of the TLS profile name. UROUTMON now makes use of network connectors, so TCP and TLS connectors can be configured in the monitor assignment file. This has the following consequences: The connection timeout, which was built-in to the previous implementation, must now be explicitly set. To prevent long delays when a connection error occurs, set the network timeout. For example, in urmon.asn : [SETTINGS]$net_timeout cct = 5 Copy You can send connector error messages to the application log file by setting $IOPRINT . If TLS is used, the UROUTMON component works only in the context of a Uniface application and cannot be called from 3GL components. If you continue to use TCP for Uniface Router Monitor connections, you don't have to make any changes to your existing monitor application. For more information, see UROUTMON and TLS Connector . TLS support has been added for SMTP and POP mail connections using UPOPMAIL The UPOPMAIL component can now create TLS connections with an SMTP or POP server. For TCP, connections over IP6 are now also supported. When using TLS when sending mails, you may need to use the SMTP AUTH LOGIN security driver to provide a user name and password. For more information, see LogonMail and LogonSMTP . UPOPMAIL component supports STARTTLS standard through the use of new SMTP security driver The new SMTP STARTTLS security driver upgrades an existing TCP connection to use TLS. It makes it possible to connect to an SMTP server using the STARTTLS protocol. This standard is required when connecting to Microsoft SMTP servers. For more information, see SMTP STARTTLS Security Driver . When sending mail, the STARTLS security driver must be the first one added to the UPOPMAIL's security context. Another security driver may be added to handle authentication. For more information, see UPOPMAIL , SetSecurity , and Security Context . Enhanced Uniface Router Monitor When starting Uniface Servers in the Router Monitor, the Start server dialog now has a Connection field, which enables you to choose the desired network connection, which can be TCP or TLS. It was previously only possible to specify this using the /dnp switch in the Command line switches field. If the /dnp switch is specified in the Command line switches field, it is overrides the value selected in the Connection field. For more information, see Start Uniface Servers from the Uniface Router Monitor . Testing and Debugging Features Debugger enhancements The Call Stack window has been enhanced to: Show the line of code that activates a ProcScript module. Allow you to jump to the code in the Debugger Browser so that you can examine the code in context. To do so, you can double-click the call stack item, or right-click and choose Show in Browser from the context menu. In addition, you can now set break points from a context menu in the Main Debugger window and in the Browser. For more information, see Call Stack and Monitoring ProcScript Modules . $procerrocontext now includes the ProcScript of the line in which the error occurred. The line number returned by the ProcScript function $procerrorcontext references a line in the compiled object, not the line of code as seen in the Script Editor. To make it easier to precisely identify the location that the error occurred, $procerrocontext has been enhanced to also return the ProcScript of the line where the error occurred. For more information, see $procerrorcontext . New assignment setting: $NO_DEBUG When $NO_DEBUG = 1 , compiling components and application shells generates corresponding runtime objects that cannot be debugged. This is useful when preparing an application for release. It is the equivalent of the /nodebug command line switch. For more information, see $NO_DEBUG . Additional options for $ude("compile") Additional options are now available for $ude("compile") , which enable you to configure the information generated during compilation. These bring the functionality into line with equivalent assignment settings and command line sub-switches, such as $NO_DEBUG and /nodebug . For more information, see Compilation and $ude ("compile") . MSAA is now supported in the TabEx widget You can use Microsoft Active Accessibility (MSAA) API when automating tests for this widget, and its child tabs and the tab strip scrollbar. For more information, see Accessibility and utabex (TabEx) . Licensing Features Customizing license expiration messages handling By default, when a Uniface license will expire within 30 days, Uniface starts displaying a hard-coded message in a dialog box whenever the application is started. Although it has always been possible to change the notification period , it is now possible to provide custom message handling in the retrieveMessage trigger or the application. A new license option is provided in the $LICENSE_OPTIONS assignment setting. By specifying the LM_NOTIFICATION_POSTMESSAGE option, Uniface suppresses the default notification message and sends the relevant expiry information to the application as an asynchronous message (postmessage), where it can be handled by code in the receiveMessage trigger. For more information, see Uniface License Management . Support for Sentinel License Manager The Uniface 10 Community Edition makes use of the Sentinel Cloud License Manager rather than the Uniface Distributed License Manager(DLM) currently used for the Enterprise Edition. License options, such as control over renewal and expiry notifications have been extended to support Sentinel. For more information, see $LICENSE_OPTIONS . New path logicals USYSLOG and USYSLIC New path logicals are now available to specify the locations of log files and local license files. You can set the path logicals USYSLOG and USYSLIC in usys.ini , for example: [paths]USYSLOG=D:/Uniface10/logUSYSLIC=D:/Uniface10/license Copy Once defined, path logicals can be used in assignment files. For example: [SETTINGS]$PRINT_OUTPUT = USYSLOG:ide_%p.log$TRANSCRIPT_LOG_FILE = USYSLOG:compile_%p.log Copy For more information, see USYS Path Logicals . Returning a license for the Community Edition If you need to install the Uniface 10 Community Edition on another machine, you can return a cloud-leased Sentinel license so that it can be reused on another machine. The shortcut in the Start menu has been renamed to License Management. For more information, see Return Sentinel Licenses for the Community Edition . Command line interface for the Uniface License Management utility The Uniface License Management utility, which is used to activate and return licenses for the Uniface Community Edition, now provides a command line interface. For more information, see Uniface License Management Utility (ulic) . Sentinel License Management is now available for new Uniface deployment installations on Linux It is now possible to use Sentinel to manage Uniface licenses on Linux platforms. For more information, see Installing Uniface on Unix and Linux , Manually Configure Sentinel Licenses on Linux , and $LICENSE_OPTIONS . If you are interested in deploying on Linux, please contact your Uniface account manager. Specifying a license file for Sentinel For Sentinel cloud licensing, if you specify the LM_LICENSE_FILE , the name of the license file must be lservrc and the directory must be the same as the one specified by USYSLIC . Otherwise, the license cannot be renewed by the Sentinel Cloud Server. For more information, see $LICENSE_OPTIONS . Other Features You now can create referential integrity constraint scripts with the /genSql command line switch. The /gensql now supports additional parameters that provide the functionality of the Create Script facility: createReferentialIntegrity , dropReferentialIntegrity , and validateReferentialIntegrity . For more information, see /genSql and Create Script Facility . UPOPMAIL component supports OAUTH 2.0 standard through the use of new security drivers for POP and SMTP When using the UPOPMAIL component to connect to SMTP and POP3 servers, you can use a security driver to handle authorization. Use the UPOPMAIL.SetSecurity operation to add a driver to the security context. For more information, see UPOPMAIL , SetSecurity , and Security Context . Uniface provides two new security drivers, one for SMTP and one for POP3, which use the OAuth 2.0 protocol to connect to mail servers using an access token instead of a password. These are provided as samples available from Rocket Community . For more information, see XOAUTH2 Security Driver . The Security API has also extended so that you can build your own OAuth security driver. For more information, see APIs: Security Driver and Security Driver Macros . Japanese language setups now support Reiwa era. The language setup for Japan has been updated with the new Japanese Era Reiwa (令和). For more information, see Japanese Era Date Formats . It is now possible to set the message severity for the compiler message #Include not found By default, if Uniface cannot find a specified IncludeScript during compilation, it issues a warning message that it cannot find the specified IncludeScript ( 1000 - (Preprocessor) #INCLUDE IncludeName Not Found ). You can now change the message severity level to error or info using the assignment setting $INCLUDE_NOT_FOUND_LEVEL or the command line switch /ilv . For more information, see $INCLUDE_NOT_FOUND_LEVEL and /ilv . Specifying the message level of compilation messages When compiling from the command line, you can specify whether to display warning and information messages (in addition to error messages) . It is now possible to specify this at a general level using the new assignment setting $MESSAGE_LEVEL . For more information, see Compilation and $MESSAGE_LEVEL . Bound to property in newly-derived component now defaults to qualified entity name When creating a new derived component, the Bound to property now contains the qualified name of the generic entity, for example MYENTITY.GENERIC instead of MYENTITY . For more information, see Generic and Bound Entities and Fields . New $ude ProcScript functions for looking up runtime resources and copying them to a UAR Use $ude("lookup") to look up existing runtime objects, symbol tables, or script listings. Use $ude("archive") to copy compiled runtime objects from the Resource Output ($RSO) path to a Uniface archive (UAR) file. For more information, see $ude ("lookup") and $ude ("archive") . New assignment setting $EXIT_TIMEOUT If you are experiencing problems with hanging processes when closing down Uniface, you can set $EXIT_TIMEOUT to kill a hanging process, and specify the length of time to wait before doing so. For more information, see $EXIT_TIMEOUT . Improved access to remote application resources It is now possible to let your application delegate the search for resources to a server. You can do this by specifying the server path in the [RESOURCES] section of your application's assignment file. For more information, see [RESOURCES] . Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
